channel.title: math.KT updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.KT updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: On the K-theory of matroids with Tutte coverings
item.description: arXiv:2603.18288v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The aim of this work is to explicitly compute the K-theory of the category of matroids with respect to the covering family of Tutte coverings. In particular, we show that this is equivalent to the K-theory spectrum of the category of graphic matroids on looped forests, with the covering family generated by isomorphisms. Further, we show that this yields an equivalence of $C_2$-spectra.
item.dc:creator: Luigi Caputi, Sabino Di Trani

item.title: The Local Lifting Property, Property FD, and stability of approximate representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.18456v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We establish Kirchberg's Local Lifting Property and Lubotzky-Shalom's Property FD for classes of finitely generated groups of central importance in geometric and combinatorial group theory: $3$-manifold groups, limit groups, and certain one-relator groups and right-angled Artin groups. We deduce that such groups are very flexibly stable, with respect to normalized unitarily invariant norms. The exposition is made accessible to operator algebraists and group theorists alike.
item.dc:creator: Francesco Fournier-Facio, Rufus Willett

item.title: On Topological Andr\'e-Quillen homology of Eilenberg-MacLane spectra
item.description: arXiv:2603.18932v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Based on the work of Dundas, Lindenstrauss and Richter we compute the topological Andr\'e-Quillen homology with reduced coefficients for Eilenberg-MacLane spectra such as $H\mathbb{Z}$ and $H\mathbb{Z}/p^n$. The case of $H\mathbb{F}_p$ was settled in an unpublished work of Basterra and Mandell, which was refined later by Brantner and Mathew in the context of spectral partition Lie algebras. Our approach is similar to Cartan's calculation of the Steenrod algebra, and eventually shorter. We also present some computations relative to the $H\mathbb{Z}$ base.
item.dc:creator: Cyril Barlasov

item.title: Methods in complete intersections in corank one
item.description: arXiv:2512.02373v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Let $A$ denote an affine algebra over an algebraically closed field $k$, with $\dim A=d\geq 3$. In the light of availability of cancellation theorems for stably free modules $P$ with $rank(P)=d-1$ (corank one), we try to implement the methods of complete intersections theory in corank zero, to the corank one case. Our conclusion is that cancellation theorems need to clean up some of the lack of minor generalities, for such an approach to work. However, we hypothesize and derive some of the consequences to complete intersections, of such hypotheses.
item.dc:creator: Satya Mandal
